# pgBelayCrew

> A control plane for PostgreSQL backups — measurable, repeatable, auditable. An operational layer around wal-g (scheduling, verification, audit) that turns untested runbooks into restores you can prove. Open source, AGPL v3.

This site is the landing page for the pgBelayCrew project. It is not the product itself — the product is a console + agents that operate alongside an existing PostgreSQL deployment and S3-compatible storage. The page exists to explain what the project is, who it is for, and how to try it.

The project's positioning is deliberately narrow: it does not replace wal-g, it wraps it. wal-g performs the backup/restore work; pgBelayCrew adds the operator-facing control plane (scheduling, verification, compliance evidence, audit log) that wal-g intentionally does not provide.

## What the project is

- Control plane for PostgreSQL backups built on top of wal-g.
- Three components: a Console (operator UI), an Agent (one per PostgreSQL host, stateless wal-g wrapper), and S3-compatible storage. Console ↔ Agent over gRPC mTLS; Agent ↔ Storage over the S3 API.
- Four primitives organize the operator's day: Cluster, Backup, Restore point, Restore run.
- Restore points come at four paranoia levels: light, enough, safe, paranoid.
- Restore runs expose four intents: sandbox, recover-data, restore-cluster, emergency. Destructive paths require dual approval.
- Verifications run at four levels (L1–L4): wal-g integrity check on the agent, sandbox restore + smoke checks, L2 plus an operator assertion suite, and PITR replay (not yet implemented — fails honestly). L1 is agent-driven; L2/L3 are console-driven and opt-in in production. Triggered on cron or after every full backup. Each emits a signed attestation.
- Configuration advisor with 15 rules sourced from official PostgreSQL and wal-g docs. Each finding includes severity, impact, and the exact `ALTER SYSTEM` to fix it.
- Hash-chained audit log; every action appended and verifiable, exportable as a signed bundle.
- Compliance dashboard: 33 controls across SOC 2, ISO/IEC 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, and PCI DSS, evaluated by real DB queries, with a gap register, waiver workflow, 90-day trend, and signed audit bundle.

## License and stance

- AGPL v3 — chosen on purpose, not as a default.
- Maintainer's stated stance: open source as a defense against enshittification of closed services; users must always be able to take the code and run their own version.
- Four tenets in the manifesto: open beats closed for new ideas; open source is a habit (every change in public), not a checkbox; money is fuel, not the goal; open source is a gift, not an invoice.

## Quickstart (as advertised on the page)

The repository ships a Docker stack — real PostgreSQL, real MinIO, real Dex (OIDC), real agents — exercising every code path end-to-end:

```
./scripts/bootstrap.sh        # dev PKI + .env
docker compose up -d          # PG + MinIO + Dex + console + agents
./scripts/enroll-agents.sh    # mint tokens, attach 3 agents
./scripts/smoke.sh            # 17 end-to-end scenarios
open http://localhost         # sign in: admin / admin
```

## Page sections

- [Hero](index.html#hero): tagline, license badge, screenshot carousel of the console (dashboard, cluster overview, restore points, verifications, configuration advisor, compliance, audit).
- [Problem](index.html#problem): four problems addressed — restoring is stressful, backups are never tested, compliance is opaque, RTO/RPO are theoretical.
- [Features](index.html#features): four primitives (Cluster, Backup, Restore point, Restore run) plus surrounding capabilities (verifications, configuration advisor, S3 backends, hash-chained audit log).
- [Architecture](index.html#architecture): three roles only — Console, Agent (×N), S3-compatible storage.
- [Compliance](index.html#compliance): SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, PCI DSS.
- [Quickstart](index.html#quickstart): five-minute Docker stack.
- [Credit](index.html#credit): explicit acknowledgment that wal-g (https://github.com/wal-g/wal-g) does the hard part.
- [Manifesto](index.html#manifesto): the maintainer's note on open source and AGPL v3.

## Files on this site

- [index.html](index.html): the full landing page (single-page, all sections inline).
- [styles.css](styles.css): styling.
- [script.js](script.js): carousel, animated constellation backdrop, minor interactivity.
- [screens/](screens/): screenshots of the actual console used in the hero carousel.

## Upstream

- wal-g: https://github.com/wal-g/wal-g — the engine pgBelayCrew wraps. Backups are wal-g backups; restores are wal-g restores.
